name: Cloud IAM Abuse
description: Attack tree modelling abuse of cloud IAM misconfigurations and privilege escalation in AWS/Azure/GCP
root_objective: Achieve cloud admin access through IAM misconfiguration exploitation

nodes.title: Achieve Cloud Admin Access
nodes.description: Escalate to cloud administrator or equivalent highly privileged role through IAM abuse

nodes.title: Obtain Initial Cloud Credentials
nodes.description: Acquire valid cloud credentials through various attack vectors

nodes.title: Steal Credentials from CI/CD Pipeline
nodes.description: Extract cloud credentials stored in CI/CD environment variables, secrets managers, or build logs

nodes.title: Exploit Instance Metadata (SSRF)
nodes.description: Use SSRF to query cloud instance metadata service and obtain IAM role temporary credentials

nodes.title: Leaked Credentials in Code Repository
nodes.description: Find cloud access keys committed to version control systems or public repositories

nodes.title: Escalate IAM Privileges
nodes.description: Abuse IAM policies and role trust relationships to escalate to higher privileges

nodes.title: Exploit Overly Permissive IAM Policy
nodes.description: Abuse iam:PassRole, sts:AssumeRole, or equivalent to escalate privileges

nodes.title: Cross-Account Role Assumption
nodes.description: Abuse misconfigured cross-account trust policies to assume roles in other AWS accounts

nodes.title: Abuse Cloud Service Misconfigurations
nodes.description: Exploit misconfigured cloud services that grant unintended access

nodes.title: Access Open S3/Blob Storage
nodes.description: Discover and access publicly exposed or misconfigured cloud storage buckets

nodes.title: Exploit Lambda/Function Permissions
nodes.description: Abuse serverless function execution roles that have excessive permissions
